edges.description: Multifactorial liability is represented upstream of intrusive, appearance-related preoccupation.
Appearance concerns are modeled as driving repetitive checking, grooming, reassurance seeking, and related behaviors.
Aberrant perceptual processing is represented as a contributor to appearance distortions and concern.
edges.source: Aberrant Visual Attention and Perceptual Processing

nodes.description: BDD involves selective attention biases and altered visual scanning that may contribute to imbalanced global versus detailed appearance processing.
nodes.id: Aberrant Visual Attention and Perceptual Processing

evidence.reference_title: Neural and behavioral effects of modification of visual attention in body dysmorphic disorder
evidence.snippet: In individuals with body dysmorphic disorder (BDD), perceptual appearance distortions may be related to selective attention biases and aberrant visual scanning, contributing to imbalances in global vs. detailed visual processing.

evidence.reference_title: Neural and behavioral effects of modification of visual attention in body dysmorphic disorder
evidence.snippet: We acquired fMRI data in 37 unmedicated adults with BDD and 30 healthy controls.

nodes.description: Persistent concern with perceived physical appearance defects.

evidence.reference_title: [Body dysmorphic disorder: Symptoms, prevalence, assessment and treatment].
evidence.snippet: Body dysmorphic disorder (BDD) is a relatively common disorder characterized by a preoccupation with nonexistent or slight defects in appearance.

nodes.description: Cognitive behavioral therapy is an evidence-supported psychological treatment for BDD symptoms and related anxiety, depression, functioning, and quality-of-life outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: [Body dysmorphic disorder: Symptoms, prevalence, assessment and treatment].
evidence.snippet: Research suggests that cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and SSRI medication are most effective for BDD.

evidence.reference_title: The efficacy of psychological treatments on body dysmorphic disorder: a meta-analysis and trial sequential analysis of randomized controlled trials
evidence.snippet: This study included 15 RCTs up until 15 June 2024, with 905 participants.

evidence.reference_title: The efficacy of psychological treatments on body dysmorphic disorder: a meta-analysis and trial sequential analysis of randomized controlled trials
evidence.snippet: In conclusion, this study underscores the effectiveness of psychological treatments in reducing BDD symptoms and improving related outcomes, highlighting the need for further research to confirm the impact of these therapies on other outcomes.

nodes.description: Repetitive checking, grooming, skin picking, reassurance seeking, or camouflaging behaviors.

evidence.reference_title: [Body dysmorphic disorder: Symptoms, prevalence, assessment and treatment].
evidence.snippet: The preoccupation with the perceived appearance defect typically occurs for many hours a day and is often followed by repetitive behaviours (for example mirror checking and skin picking).

nodes.description: BDD is modeled as arising from interacting biological, psychological, and environmental factors that produce persistent appearance concerns and repetitive responses to perceived defects.

evidence.reference_title: Pharmacological Treatment of Body Dysmorphic Disorder.
evidence.snippet: It is currently understood to arise from a combination of biological, psychological, and environmental factors.

nodes.description: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors are represented as pharmacotherapy for BDD, usually alongside CBT in current treatment approaches.

evidence.reference_title: [Body dysmorphic disorder: Symptoms, prevalence, assessment and treatment].
evidence.snippet: Research suggests that cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and SSRI medication are most effective for BDD.

evidence.reference_title: Pharmacological Treatment of Body Dysmorphic Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Treatment of body dysmorphic disorder typically consists of a combination of pharmacotherapy and cognitive behavioral therapy.
